data.epi_content:
It's morning.

I haven't slept for days now.

Two days, maybe.

I can manage.

But eating nothing but chocolate for days is really getting difficult now.

How did I even manage back then?

I was confused after becoming this young lady, and she was practically insane and trying to die, so I guess I didn't have the mental space to worry about such things.

Well, animals like cats tend to run to small, cozy places while gasping for breath when they're sick or about to die.

Maybe this young lady is feeling something similar.

Though she doesn't seem much like a cat.

I've set a slightly different goal.

Last time it was suicide, which was a failure. So this time, I'll try a different method of dying.

If it's impossible for me to kill myself—or more precisely, if the problem is that I keep coming back even after dying—

Maybe having someone else kill me could solve the problem.

It's a simple thought without a shred of evidence or logic, but I don't particularly feel like pointing that out to myself.

That's how desperate I might be.

"Only eight left."

I load six bullets into the gun.

The remaining two, I can use them somehow later.

I'll put them in my pocket.

Come to think of it, I hadn't figured out how to smuggle the gun into the classroom.

All the items in my room weren't brought by me, but moved here by servants employed by the family.

If someone asked what I brought personally, I could maybe explain away coffee beans, but anyway.

How should I bring this?

Maybe hide it inside a thick book?

With that thought, I pull a thick history book from the bookshelf.

It was too bothersome to carve out the pages with a knife or something, so I just roughly tore off the outer cover.

Though I struggled quite a bit due to my lack of strength.

I feel dizzy after suddenly moving my body.

I brew a cup of coffee and put a bunch of angular chocolate pieces in it.

The chocolate melts into a hideous form, making the already black color even darker.

If I just whip this cup now, maybe it'll diligently bring sugar on its own.

It might even bring cotton because it's so stupid.

"Heheh."

Making these nonsensical thoughts helps calm my trembling hands a bit.

I drink the coffee with melted chocolate, place the gun between the book covers, and look in the mirror.

A slightly flushed face.

Dark circles so large they almost reach my mouth.

If before I looked coldly ruthless, now I look messy and weak.

Perhaps it's natural, since I'm drifting around like a raft on the ocean without any sense of purpose.

Or maybe "being pushed around" would be more accurate.

Whatever, it doesn't matter.

If I failed once, I can just try again.

I don't know when I became such a positive person, but somehow I feel like I need to change like this as the end approaches.

What was it again?

I don't remember well.

They say people who nearly died and survived realize the preciousness of life and start living earnestly again.

That's it. That's it.
data.epi_content2:
If someone says that's different—

Those people focus on living more earnestly.

I'm just putting in a little effort to die again.

Let's just say the direction is slightly different.

Today I leave my room a bit earlier than usual.

I wanted to shower, but my body wasn't dirty, I didn't feel uncomfortable, and I hadn't sweated, so I skipped it.

Trying to walk naturally while being conscious of the phrase "as usual" actually makes me feel more unnatural.

My legs were creaking, and my eyes kept losing focus and blurring repeatedly.

I don't know if it's because of stress or lack of proper sleep.

Anyway, I successfully managed to walk into the classroom with the gun.

And there was no one there.

I put the gun in the drawer for now.

Then the students start coming in one by one.

Lydia looks at me, frowns slightly, then smiles.

Then she waves her hand slightly as if to say she'll see me later.

When the teacher enters the classroom, the class flows as usual.

A daily routine where those who listen attend the lesson while the rest gather and play.

But there was something out of place there.

An object that would either protect my body or easily help destroy this child's body.

Eventually, the class ends, and the teacher, having received some message from Lydia, calls out the students who were listening and tells them to follow him to the faculty office.

Then he starts leaving the classroom in a hurry.

Only then, as the students whose names were called look confused and start moving, does Lydia approach me.

Her face was somewhat detestable, or like looking at a high-maintenance puppy.

"Miss, I see you escaped from that small place last time.

Well, I guess some kind person unlocked it for you? Who opened it for you?"

Come to think of it, could Lydia use magic?

I can't remember well.

Seeing how she always has kids do her bidding, maybe she herself can't do much.

"Girls, stand behind her in case the miss tries to go somewhere else."

At her words, two girls a head taller than me walk behind me.

I couldn't run properly anyway, so why worry about me escaping already?

If anything, I'd like to escape to heaven.

Hell might be fine too.

How much worse could it be than the human world, no matter how terrible?

Who knows, maybe demons ruling hell had a rebellion and created a separate afterlife for humans.

Anyway, that would make things much easier.

Just look at how she's handling these children.

"Miss? I'm asking who let you out.

I thought you'd become a bit more docile, but why aren't you answering?"

"Why should I answer—"

Slap!

Lydia lightly twisted her waist and struck my cheek.

Is this the second time, or not? I'm not sure.

Had Lydia hit me before?

Well, it doesn't really matter.

There was a better option in the desk drawer than suffering this humiliation, crying, and cursing Lydia inside.

I pretend to be shocked from being slapped for the first time.

And that was quite easy.

With dark circles from not sleeping for two days, and feeling so weak that I could barely see straight, I just had to stare blankly in the direction my face had turned with my mouth open.
data.epi_content3:
"Hey, Lydia."

"Yes. I'm sorry for hitting you, so I'll answer."

"Why am I alive?

Have you ever thought about why you're alive?"

I take something out from below.

Lydia's expression begins to change.

"Erica miss...?"

I pull out the gun I had put in the desk drawer and cock the hammer.

With a click-clack sound, the bullet is ready to fire at any moment.

Thinking I might not be able to kill her, or more precisely, because of my experience of shooting myself in the neck and not dying instantly, I press the muzzle against Lydia's forehead.

Our eyes meet.

Her pupils shake frantically, and she's too shocked to say anything.

Also, she seems too frightened to run away even if she wanted to.

"First, let me—"

Bang!

A bullet fired at close range pierces through a girl's forehead.

The recoil made my right wrist tingle slightly after firing.

What was once standing, once called a girl named Lydia, loses balance and falls in the direction the bullet traveled.

And simultaneously, screams erupt.

After staring at Lydia, who lies with eyes open and small fountains of blood spurting from her head, I point the gun at the screaming child and ask:

"Why did you come behind me?"

"Lydia told us to keep you from running away... I'm sorry! I'm sorry!"

She starts making excuses but trails off and begins apologizing.

Then, without being told, she kneels and starts wringing her hands.

"My heart told me to."

So I smiled at the girl.

While pulling the trigger.

The girl who had been kneeling and begging to someone unknown falls forward.

Blood soaked into my shoes.

The lukewarm wetness and the distinctive smell of blood made me feel sick.

Because of that, my desire to do anything disappeared, and I started thinking maybe I should just blow my own head off with this thing I'm holding.

Taking advantage of that moment, the remaining students and teacher turned their backs and fled.

I fired the remaining four shots randomly, but even those who were slightly hit in the back were fine and ran away holding their waists.

I guess adrenaline or something kept them from collapsing.

I'm envious.

I pull out a chair from a nearby desk and sit down again.

I empty all the spent casings with a rattling sound and load the remaining two bullets from my pocket.

As expected of the Academy.

They handle situations very quickly.

Someone with blonde hair, wearing the same uniform as the students but looking exceptionally special, enters the classroom.

"What... is this...?"

I point the gun at this person who looks confused and pull the trigger.

And then, I wonder why no bullet comes out no matter how many times I pull it. Then I realize why.

My right hand has disappeared.
